goal: Build a mixed-SKU sorted palletizer: a UR10 robot picks two box types (SKU-A: small 0.08m red cubes, SKU-B: large 0.12m blue cubes) arriving interleaved on an infeed conveyor, and places them into separate pallet zones (ZoneA left half, ZoneB right half) on a single 1.0×0.50m pallet. Semantic labels 'sku_a' and 'sku_b' are applied at scene creation; add_vision_classifier_gate classifies each box at the pick sensor; setup_pick_place_controller with color_routing dispatches to the correct zone. Each zone's 2×3 drop targets are pre-baked inline (zone geometry partitions the pallet at the x=0 midline). Exercises the n_bins_routed sort pattern on UR10 with two asymmetric pallet zones — the minimum distinguishing step beyond the uniform 2×3 single-zone layer-stack canonical.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-palletizer-mixed-sku:

1) Distinguishing feature: this canonical extends the uniform-SKU layer-stack (CP-NEW-palletizer-layer-stack) by routing two box types to spatially separate pallet zones. The key new schema element is color_routing on setup_pick_place_controller — the same mechanism used in sorter-color-3lane but applied to pallet zones rather than discrete bins.

2) Robot choice: UR10 (reach 1.3m), mounted on a 0.75m table. Pallet centre at y=-0.55, x=0.0; ZoneA centre at x=-0.25, ZoneB centre at x=+0.25. Maximum horizontal reach = sqrt(0.25^2 + 0.55^2) = 0.605m — well inside 1.3m. Conveyor at y=+0.45.

3) Box geometry:
   - SKU-A (red, 0.08m): lighter parts. Belt top 0.805; box centre z = 0.805 + 0.04 = 0.845.
   - SKU-B (blue, 0.12m): larger parts. Box centre z = 0.805 + 0.06 = 0.865.
   - Conveyor carries mixed order: A, B, A, B, A, B (3 of each = 6 total).
   - All start at x=-1.6..-0.6, y=0.45, z=appropriate for their size.

4) Pallet layout: 1.0m wide × 0.50m deep × 0.05m thick at [0, -0.55, 0.775].
   Pallet top z = 0.775 + 0.025 = 0.800.
   ZoneA (left, x<0): SKU-A 0.08m boxes. drop_z = 0.800 + 0.04 = 0.840.
     Grid 1×3 at x=-0.25, y offsets = {-0.47, -0.55, -0.63}, col offsets = {-0.06, +0.06}.
     Actually 2×3 in zone half: x in {-0.34, -0.16}, y in {-0.47, -0.55, -0.63}.
   ZoneB (right, x>0): SKU-B 0.12m boxes. drop_z = 0.800 + 0.06 = 0.860.
     2×3 at x in {+0.09, +0.41}, y in {-0.47, -0.55, -0.63}.
   Spacing between zone edges: ZoneA rightmost x=+0.16+0.04=+0.20, ZoneB leftmost x=+0.09-0.06=+0.03. Tight but non-overlapping given asymmetric box sizes.
   Simplified to 1 column per zone (3 drops each, 2 cols squeezed): use x={-0.30,-0.20} for A-zone and x={+0.20,+0.30} for B-zone.

5) Semantic labels: 'sku_a' (red) and 'sku_b' (blue). color_routing keys must exactly match labels used in add_vision_classifier_gate destination_map.

6) Classification camera overhead at [0, 0.45, 1.40], looking at pick sensor [0.35, 0.45, 0.855].

7) color_routing keys 'sku_a'→'/World/PalletZoneA' and 'sku_b'→'/World/PalletZoneB'. ZoneA/ZoneB are thin invisible xform-platform prims acting as destination_path anchors; drop_targets dict overrides the actual per-box positions.

8) Physics: CPU dynamics (MBP broadphase) for determinism. All boxes get sleepThreshold=0.0.

9) EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT = max(box_on_belt, max_drop_z) + 0.20 = max(0.865, 0.860) + 0.20 = 1.065m. Consistent across both SKUs.

10) pick_sensor at [0.35, 0.45, 0.855] — centroid between SKU-A (z=0.845) and SKU-B (z=0.865) boxes; detection window 0.14×0.14×0.14 captures both.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1200.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for determinism ────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Robot table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── UR10 robot (reach 1.3m) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="ur10",
    dest_path="/World/UR10",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Infeed conveyor ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Belt top z = 0.78 + 0.025 = 0.805. Mixed A/B boxes carried at 0.12 m/s.
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.45, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.12, 0, 0],
)

# ── Materials ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_material(material_path="/World/Looks/MatRed",  shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.85, 0.10, 0.10])
create_material(material_path="/World/Looks/MatBlue", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.10, 0.20, 0.85])

# ── Mixed boxes: 3×SKU-A (0.08m, red) + 3×SKU-B (0.12m, blue), interleaved ──
# SKU-A: centre z = 0.805 + 0.04 = 0.845
# SKU-B: centre z = 0.805 + 0.06 = 0.865
_sku_sequence = ["a", "b", "a", "b", "a", "b"]
_sku_size = {"a": 0.08, "b": 0.12}
_sku_z    = {"a": 0.845, "b": 0.865}
_sku_mat  = {"a": "/World/Looks/MatRed", "b": "/World/Looks/MatBlue"}
_sku_label = {"a": "sku_a", "b": "sku_b"}
_x_positions = [-1.60, -1.35, -1.10, -0.85, -0.60, -0.35]
box_paths = []

for i, sku in enumerate(_sku_sequence):
    path = f"/World/Box_{i+1}"
    box_paths.append(path)
    create_prim(
        prim_path=path,
        prim_type="Cube",
        position=[_x_positions[i], 0.45, _sku_z[sku]],
        size=_sku_size[sku],
    )
    assign_material(prim_path=path, material_path=_sku_mat[sku])
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name=_sku_label[sku])
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=box_paths,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in box_paths:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# ── Pallet (1.0×0.50×0.05m) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# scale half-extents: [0.50, 0.25, 0.025]. Pallet top z = 0.775+0.025 = 0.800.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Pallet",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, -0.55, 0.775],
    scale=[0.50, 0.25, 0.025],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Pallet", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Zone anchor prims (invisible Xform) for color_routing dispatch ────────────
# ZoneA: x<0 (left half), ZoneB: x>0 (right half). No collision needed.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/PalletZoneA", prim_type="Xform", position=[-0.25, -0.55, 0.800])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/PalletZoneB", prim_type="Xform", position=[ 0.25, -0.55, 0.800])

# ── Classification camera ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/ClassifyCamera", prim_type="Camera", position=[0, 0.45, 1.40])
set_camera_look_at(
    camera_path="/World/ClassifyCamera",
    target=[0.35, 0.45, 0.855],
    up=[0, 0, 1],
)

# ── Proximity sensor at pick station ─────────────────────────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    position=[0.35, 0.45, 0.855],
    size=[0.14, 0.14, 0.14],
    watched_path_pattern="/World/Box_",
)

# ── Vision classifier gate ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
add_vision_classifier_gate(
    cube_paths=box_paths,
    class_labels=["sku_a", "sku_b"],
    camera_path="/World/ClassifyCamera",
    destination_map={
        "sku_a": "/World/PalletZoneA",
        "sku_b": "/World/PalletZoneB",
    },
)

# ── Pre-computed zone drop targets ────────────────────────────────────────────
# ZoneA: SKU-A boxes (0.08m) → drop_z = 0.800 + 0.04 = 0.840
#   2 cols × 3 rows: x in {-0.30, -0.20}, y in {-0.47, -0.55, -0.63}
# ZoneB: SKU-B boxes (0.12m) → drop_z = 0.800 + 0.06 = 0.860
#   2 cols × 3 rows: x in {+0.20, +0.30}, y in {-0.47, -0.55, -0.63}
drop_targets = {
    "/World/Box_1": [-0.30, -0.47, 0.840],
    "/World/Box_2": [ 0.20, -0.47, 0.860],
    "/World/Box_3": [-0.20, -0.47, 0.840],
    "/World/Box_4": [ 0.30, -0.47, 0.860],
    "/World/Box_5": [-0.30, -0.55, 0.840],
    "/World/Box_6": [ 0.20, -0.55, 0.860],
}

# ── cuRobo pick-place controller with 2-SKU color routing ────────────────────
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/UR10",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="ur10",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=box_paths,
    destination_path="/World/PalletZoneA",
    color_routing={
        "sku_a": "/World/PalletZoneA",
        "sku_b": "/World/PalletZoneB",
    },
    drop_targets=drop_targets,
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Pallet"],
    end_effector_initial_height=1.065,
)
failure_modes: color_routing key mismatch — class_name in set_semantic_label ('sku_a'/'sku_b') must EXACTLY match color_routing dict keys AND add_vision_classifier_gate class_labels. All three must be consistent; any capitalisation or spacing difference causes all boxes to fall back to destination_path.

Mixed drop_z values with shared pick sensor — SKU-A drop_z=0.840 and SKU-B drop_z=0.860 differ by 0.02m; end_effector_initial_height=1.065 clears both. If cuRobo fails for one SKU but not the other, check that EE height is not being auto-computed per-SKU incorrectly.

ZoneA/ZoneB Xform prims have no collision mesh — color_routing dispatches via prim path only; destination_path fallback '/World/PalletZoneA' will not obstruct EE. If planner reports no collision objects missing, this is expected.

UR10 robot_family not registered — if robot_family='ur10' raises KeyError in curobo handler, fall back to omitting the arg (handler auto-detects from robot_path USD asset name). Confirm ur10 is in ROBOT_FAMILY_CONFIGS.

Belt speed misaligned — at 0.12 m/s Box_6 (x=-0.35, 1.25m to sensor at x=0.35) arrives after ~10s; if the prior 5 cycles are slow, Box_6 may overshoot. Reduce belt to 0.08 m/s if the last box is missed.

workpieces.note: must have equal counts of each SKU type; odd-indexed are SKU-A, even-indexed are SKU-B
